title: myrmecodia schlechteri subsp. kopiagoensis
intro:
Myrmecodia schlechteri subsp. kopiagoensis, commonly known as the "Kopiago Mantis Shrimp Orchid" or "Kopiago Ant Plant," is a rare and unique epiphytic plant species endemic to Papua New Guinea, characterized by its distinctive, globular shape and modified leaves that form a "cup" to attract and house ants for mutualistic protection.

This plant is believed to possess medicinal properties due to its unique compounds and chemical composition, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits. Some reported health benefits of the plant include its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and inflammatory diseases. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are traditionally used in folk medicine by local communities in Papua New Guinea.
